message:
fix(extensions_js): correct AbortSignal polyfill reason handling for abort() and timeout()

Three fixes to the AbortSignal polyfill in the JS runtime shim:

1. reason getter: Now checks this._reason before falling back to the
   default "This operation was aborted" error. Previously, even when
   AbortSignal.abort(reason) set _reason on the instance, the getter
   ignored it and always returned the generic message — making custom
   abort reasons invisible to consumer code.

2. AbortSignal.abort(reason): Now explicitly sets _reason to the
   provided reason (or the default error when reason is undefined).
   Previously, _reason was set to the raw reason value which could be
   undefined, causing throwIfAborted() to throw undefined instead of
   a proper Error object.

3. AbortSignal.timeout(ms): Now sets _reason to a timeout-specific
   error message ("The operation was aborted due to timeout") when the
   timeout fires. Previously, _reason was never set on timeout, so
   signal.reason returned the generic abort message rather than
   indicating that the abort was caused by a timeout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
